phases.name: Parser foundation: WorkloadSpec discriminator + Schedule + Service listeners
phases.description: Lands the WorkloadSpec tagged enum (Service / Job / Schedule) at the parser boundary per ADR-0047 §1, plus the Slice 05 Schedule parsing/deferral surface and the Slice 06 Service [[listener]] spec shape. Includes the dst-lint "live"-literal grep gate (Slice 01 / US-06) and the examples/coinflip.toml migration to [job] shape (Slice 01 / US-07). Mapping note: the user-supplied default mapping is followed for slices 01 / 05 / 06. Step 01-03 (slice-06 Service listeners) declares a cross-phase dep on step 03-01 (slice-04 Service preservation) because slice-06's submit-echo + alloc-status render extension extends the Service render branch whose vocabulary slice-04 owns. The DELIVER runner schedules on step deps, not phase-id ordering, so the actual execution sequence is 01-01 → 03-01 → 01-02 → 02-01 → 02-03 → 02-04 → 02-05 → 02-06 → 02-07 → 02-02 → 01-03 (01-03 depends on 02-02 per its deps array, so it tails the order).

steps.name: Slice 01 — WorkloadSpec parser discriminator + dst-lint live gate + coinflip migration
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from slice-01-parser-kind-discriminator.md + ADR-0047 §1, §2 + design/wave-decisions.md [D1], [D3], [D6]; scenarios from distill/test-scenarios.md §1, §6, §7:
- crates/overdrive-core/src/aggregate/mod.rs: introduce the WorkloadSpec tagged enum with three variants Service(ServiceSpec), Job(JobSpec), Schedule(ScheduleSpec) per ADR-0047 §1 / [D1]. Existing Job struct renamed to JobSpec (single-cut rename per feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md; replicas field removed because Job kind is run-to-completion). New ServiceSpec carries the existing Job's replicas field. ScheduleSpec carries job_inner: JobSpec + cron_expr: CronExpr (where CronExpr is a Phase-1 String-shaped newtype that validates non-empty; richer cron validation is GH #166).
- WorkloadSpecInput wire shape mirrors the aggregate as a custom Deserialize impl per ADR-0047 §2 / [D3] (NOT bare #[serde(untagged)] — error messages must name the offending TOML sections). Section-as-discriminator: [service] alone → Service; [job] alone → Job; [job]+[schedule] → Schedule. Mixed [service]+[job], [schedule] alone, [schedule]+[service], or missing [exec] all reject with structured ParseError naming the offending sections.
- JobId reused across all three kinds per ADR-0047 §1a / [D6] — no WorkloadId rename, no compat shim. The CLI verb stays `overdrive job submit`.
- xtask/src/dst_lint.rs: extend the banned-API scanner with rule "live-literal-banned" — flag any source file under crates/overdrive-cli/src/render.rs or crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/ that contains the literal `"live"` outside comments / docstrings / #[cfg(test)] modules. Same scanner pattern as existing rules (per slice-01 risks section + design [D7] §xtask::dst_lint).
- examples/coinflip.toml: rewrite from the legacy flat shape to [job] + [exec] + [resources] per US-07. Single-cut migration; no compat path, no deprecation comment.
- All four input types derive serde::{Serialize, Deserialize} + utoipa::ToSchema (per ADR-0047 §1 / design wave Reuse Analysis line). WorkloadSpec aggregate derives rkyv::{Archive, Serialize, Deserialize}.

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md §1, §6, §7):
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/workload_spec_parser.rs::S_01_01_service_spec_recognised_by_section_presence — [service] alone → Service kind
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/workload_spec_parser.rs::S_01_02_job_spec_recognised_by_section_presence — [job] alone → Job kind
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/workload_spec_parser.rs::S_01_03_scheduled_job_recognised_by_co_presence — [job]+[schedule] → Schedule kind
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/workload_spec_parser.rs::S_01_04_mixed_service_job_rejected_with_named_guidance — error names both [service] and [job]
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/workload_spec_parser.rs::S_01_05_schedule_without_job_rejected — error names missing [job]
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/workload_spec_parser.rs::S_01_06_schedule_with_service_rejected — same guidance
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/workload_spec_parser.rs::S_01_07_missing_exec_rejected
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/workload_spec_parser.rs::S_01_09_mixed_kind_rejected_across_orderings — proptest 1024 cases
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/coinflip_migration.rs::S_07_01_migrated_coinflip_parses_as_job_kind — exercises the migrated examples/coinflip.toml
- xtask/tests/dst_lint_live_literal.rs::S_06_01_dst_lint_rejects_live_literal
- xtask/tests/dst_lint_live_literal.rs::S_06_02_dst_lint_allows_live_in_comments
- xtask/tests/dst_lint_live_literal.rs::S_06_03_dst_lint_passes_on_migrated_codebase

Quality gates:
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --features integration-tests for the new acceptance files
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace -E 'binary(dst_lint_live_literal)'
- cargo run -p xtask -- dst-lint exits 0 on the migrated workspace (S-06-03)
- KPI K2: 95% of mixed-kind rejection paths complete within 50 ms per spec (S-01-08)
steps.description: Slice 01 of the feature. Lands the WorkloadSpec tagged enum at the parser boundary as the new abstraction every downstream slice depends on. Single-cut renames Job → JobSpec / ServiceSpec aggregate split; introduces the custom Deserialize impl that produces named-section parser errors (NOT bare #[serde(untagged)]); ships the dst-lint "live"-literal grep gate as the structural regression guard for slices 02 and 03; migrates examples/coinflip.toml to [job] shape. The structural fix lands here — without WorkloadSpec, every later slice is blocked. No deps. ~1 day.
steps.implementation_notes: Slice mapping: slice-01-parser-kind-discriminator.md → 01-01. Stories: US-01 (parser kind discriminator) + US-06 (live grep gate) + US-07 (coinflip migration). The CronExpr newtype lands here (string-only validation; Phase-1 honest deferral); richer cron syntax is GH #166. Single-cut migration discipline per .claude/rules/development.md § Deletion discipline — no compat shim, no #[serde(alias)] for the old flat shape.

steps.name: Slice 05 — [schedule] parses with deferral URL constant + Schedule submit/alloc-status render
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from slice-05-schedule-parsing.md + ADR-0047 §1 (Schedule variant) + design/wave-decisions.md Constraints (deferral URL constants); scenarios from distill/test-scenarios.md §5:
- crates/overdrive-cli/src/render.rs (or a new module crates/overdrive-cli/src/render/schedule.rs): introduce the SCHEDULE_EXECUTION_TRACKING_URL CLI constant equal to "https://github.com/overdrive-sh/overdrive/issues/166" byte-for-byte. Every render path that names the deferral reads from this constant — submit echo and alloc status MUST NOT hardcode the URL inline (per design Constraints + KPI K5 byte-equality assertion).
- Submit echo for Schedule kind: prints "Submitting schedule '<id>' (kind=Schedule)", spec digest, endpoint, then "Schedule registered." plus the multi-line NOTE block referencing the constant. CLI exits 0 — Schedule submit is accepted; execution deferral is not a failure.
- Alloc status render for Schedule kind: prints "kind: Schedule", echoes the cron expression unchanged (string-as-written, no canonicalisation), "No allocations have been spawned yet", and a Reason line that byte-equals the submit-echo deferral URL.
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/streaming.rs: ScheduleSubmitEvent enum with Accepted + Registered { cron, deferral_url } variants per ADR-0047 §3 / [D7]. The Schedule streaming sub-path emits Accepted + Registered immediately at submit; the stream closes (no firing semantics this slice — that is GH #166).
- IntentStore: WorkloadSpec::Schedule rkyv-serialised + persisted to canonical IntentKey per J-OPS-002 ("submitted things are committed even if execution is deferred").
- examples/nightly-backup.toml: ship a [job]+[schedule] example with cron = "0 2 * * *" exercising the Schedule kind end-to-end (used by integration test).

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md §5):
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit_schedule.rs::S_05_01_submit_echoes_registered_with_deferral_url
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit_schedule.rs::S_05_02_alloc_status_byte_equality_with_submit_url — KPI K5 byte-equality
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit_schedule.rs::S_05_03_schedule_without_job_cli_handler_rejects — cross-ref S-01-05
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/schedule_parser.rs::S_05_04_schedule_requires_non_empty_cron_field
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit_schedule.rs::S_05_05_schedule_submit_persists_to_intent_store — adapter-integration
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit_schedule.rs::S_05_06_deferral_url_sourced_from_single_constant

Quality gates:
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --features integration-tests -E 'binary(job_submit_schedule)'
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --features integration-tests -E 'test(schedule_parser)'
- KPI K5: byte-equal deferral URL across submit echo and alloc status; the constant is the single SSOT (S-05-06)
steps.description: Slice 05 of the feature. Lands the Schedule-kind end-to-end surface: parser supports [schedule] alongside [job] with non-empty cron validation; submit echo and alloc-status render print an honest deferral notice referencing GH #166 from a single SCHEDULE_EXECUTION_TRACKING_URL constant; Schedule specs are persisted to IntentStore (per J-OPS-002 — submitted == committed). Cron firing semantics, ConcurrencyPolicy, history retention are all OUT OF SCOPE — tracked at GH #166. KPI K5 (byte-equal deferral URL) is the load-bearing assertion. Depends on 01-01 (WorkloadSpec must exist). ~1 day.

steps.name: Slice 06 — Service [[listener]] spec shape + submit/alloc-status listener render + OpenAPI
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from slice-06-service-listener-fields.md + ADR-0047 §1 (Service listener fields sub-section) + design/wave-decisions.md [D5], [D8] + GH #164 converged decisions; scenarios from distill/test-scenarios.md §8:
- crates/overdrive-core/src/aggregate/mod.rs: Listener type at the spec module boundary carrying { port: NonZeroU16, protocol: Proto, vip: Option<ServiceVip> }. Proto is the existing overdrive-core::Proto newtype (case-insensitive parsing, lowercase canonical render) — NOT a second Protocol enum (per design Constraints). ServiceVip is a thin newtype over Ipv4Addr; absent value is None.
- TOML deserialisation: [[listener]] is a top-level array-of-tables alongside [service] (NOT nested inside [service]) per #164 converged decision. ServiceSpec carries listeners: Vec<Listener> in declaration order.
- Parser validation rules: Service spec MUST carry at least one [[listener]]; no two listeners may share (vip, port, protocol) — when both vip are None, comparison is on (port, protocol) only; protocol is one of tcp / udp (case-insensitive); port=0 rejected; sctp / icmp / empty rejected with named guidance ("supported protocols: tcp, udp").
- crates/overdrive-cli/src/render.rs: extend the Service submit echo with a Listeners section, one line per listener formatted "<vip-or-pending>:<port>/<protocol>". Pinned VIP renders as IPv4 dotted-quad; None VIP renders as "(vip: pending allocation — see #167)" sourced from a single CLI constant SERVICE_VIP_ALLOCATOR_TRACKING_URL equal byte-for-byte to https://github.com/overdrive-sh/overdrive/issues/167.
- Service alloc-status render (extends the Service branch from 02-02): adds a Listeners section mirroring submit-echo semantics. KPI K6 byte-equality between submit echo and alloc status listener rendering is asserted.
- crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs: AllocStatusRow gains listeners: Vec<ListenerRow> embedded on the row (NOT a separate service_listener table) per ADR-0047 §4a / [D5]. ListenerRow shape { port: NonZeroU16, protocol: Proto, vip: Option<ServiceVip> } — observation-side twin of the intent-side Listener per ADR-0011's intent-vs-observation distinctness.
- utoipa::ToSchema derives on Listener and ServiceVip; OverdriveApi macro registration list grows; cargo openapi-gen regenerates api/openapi.yaml as the new golden.

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md §8):
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/listener_parser.rs::S_08_01_two_listeners_preserved_in_declaration_order
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/listener_parser.rs::S_08_02_protocol_case_insensitive_canonical_lowercase
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/listener_parser.rs::S_08_03_zero_listeners_rejected
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/listener_parser.rs::S_08_04_duplicate_triple_rejected
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/listener_parser.rs::S_08_05_unsupported_protocol_rejected
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/listener_parser.rs::S_08_06_port_zero_rejected
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit_service_listeners.rs::S_08_07_submit_echo_renders_listeners_pinned_and_pending
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit_service_listeners.rs::S_08_08_alloc_status_renders_listeners_section
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit_service_listeners.rs::S_08_09_listener_byte_equality_round_trip — KPI K6 property test
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/listener_roundtrip.rs::S_08_10_jobspec_input_roundtrips_through_toml_json_job — proptest
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/openapi_listener.rs::S_08_11_openapi_includes_listener_with_optional_vip
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit_service_listeners.rs::S_08_12_vip_url_sourced_from_single_constant

Quality gates:
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --features integration-tests -E 'binary(listener_parser) | binary(listener_roundtrip)'
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --features integration-tests -E 'binary(job_submit_service_listeners)'
- cargo openapi-gen + cargo openapi-check both exit 0 (S-08-11)
- KPI K6 byte-equality property test passes 1024 cases
steps.description: Slice 06 of the feature. Ships the Service [[listener]] spec shape (port, protocol, optional VIP) end-to-end: Listener / ServiceVip newtypes; parser deserialisation + uniqueness validation; submit-echo and alloc-status Listeners sections sourced from a single SERVICE_VIP_ALLOCATOR_TRACKING_URL constant; AllocStatusRow.listeners denormalisation per [D5]; OpenAPI derives + property test. Runtime VIP allocator behaviour for vip=None is OUT OF SCOPE — tracked at GH #167. Hard deps: 01-01 (WorkloadSpec / Service variant), 03-01 (slice-04 Service preservation owns the format_running_summary vocabulary the listener echo extends), 02-02 (slice-03 alloc-status kind-aware machinery the listener section extends). ~1.5 days.

phases.name: Job lifecycle: terminal verdict closes the bug + alloc-status kind-aware Job render
phases.description:
Closes the bug under audit by replacing flat SubmitEvent with per-kind enums (ServiceSubmitEvent / JobSubmitEvent / ScheduleSubmitEvent) per ADR-0047 §3 / [D2]. JobSubmitEvent has NO ConvergedRunning variant — making the bug structurally unreachable for Job kind. Slice 03 (alloc-status Job render) extends the kind-aware render machinery to surface verdicts + per-attempt exit codes for failed Jobs (the user's explicit framing journey). KPI K1 honesty (≥99% over 100 trials) lands here as the single Tier-3 Lima-gated test.

**Amended 2026-05-10**: the original slice-02 step (02-01) decomposed into the structural-fix step (02-01, landed in commits f910dba + 0d5a4c2) plus five runtime sub-steps (02-03..02-07) that close the remaining in-AC items surfaced after the structural fix landed. Slice-03 (02-02) deps adjusted to depend on the new sub-steps that produce the AllocStatusRow shape and typed-terminal observations it renders. Execution sequence inside this phase: 02-01 → 02-03 → 02-04 → 02-05 → 02-06 → 02-07 → 02-02. The DES log already records 02-01 as PASS under the re-scoped definition; no log edits needed.

steps.name: Slice 02 (structural fix) — JobSubmitEvent variants without ConvergedRunning + render fns + WorkloadSpec discriminator wiring
steps.criteria:
**Re-scoped 2026-05-10** to the structural fix actually landed in commits f910dba + 0d5a4c2. Runtime end-to-end items previously bundled here (typed terminal, reconciler natural-exit emission, stderr-tail capture, server-side dispatch + CLI migration, Tier-3 K1 trial run) are decomposed into sub-steps 02-03..02-07.

AC for THIS step (structural fix only):
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/streaming.rs: introduce JobSubmitEvent enum with variants Accepted, Pending, Running, AttemptFailed, Succeeded, Failed. JobSubmitEvent MUST NOT carry a ConvergedRunning variant — this is the structural fix per ADR-0047 §3 / [D2]. ServiceSubmitEvent retains its existing ConvergedRunning shape (preserved on the Service code path only). ScheduleSubmitEvent already exists from 01-02.
- crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/job.rs: WorkloadSpecInput discriminator wired at the submit handler boundary — a [job] TOML routes through new submit_streaming_job + consume_stream_job functions distinct from the Service code path. The CLI entry point reads the kind discriminator before opening any stream.
- crates/overdrive-cli/src/render.rs: render functions land as pure-function stubs operating on JobSubmitEvent variants — format_job_submit_echo, format_job_succeeded_summary, format_job_failed_summary, format_job_attempt_failed. These do NOT yet wire to a terminal observation row (that is 02-04) and do NOT yet render a real stderr tail (that is 02-05); they format whatever the variant payload carries today.
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/acceptance/job_kind_render.rs: 5 unit-level tests pinning the render fns at the pure-function boundary, including the S-02-05 anti-scenario (no Job-kind output line contains "is running with" or "(took live)") asserted at the render boundary directly.
- The literal "live" remains forbidden in render-path source — dst-lint gate from 01-01 stays green.

Scenarios claimed by THIS step:
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/acceptance/job_kind_render.rs::S_02_05_anti_scenario_no_is_running_with — anti-scenario asserted at the render-fn boundary (the pure-function manifestation of [D2]'s structural mandate)
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/acceptance/job_kind_render.rs::S_02_06_submit_echo_names_kind_upfront — unit-level pinning of format_job_submit_echo output

Scenarios deferred to later sub-steps:
- S-02-01, S-02-02, S-02-03, S-02-04, S-02-07, S-02-08 → 02-06 (server-side dispatch + CLI migration to JobSubmitEvent decode unblocks the streaming integration tests)
- S-02-09 (K1 100-trial honesty) → 02-07

Quality gates:
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli -E 'binary(job_kind_render)' — the 5 unit tests pass GREEN
- cargo run -p xtask -- dst-lint exits 0 ("live" literal grep gate from 01-01 stays green)
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run — compile-clean across the workspace under the new variant shape

Deliverable status: COMPLETE. Commits f910dba ("feat(workload-kind-discriminator): land WorkloadSpec tagged enum + dst-lint live gate + coinflip migration") and 0d5a4c2 capture the landed work. The DES log entry recording 5/5 PASS under the prior step definition is ACCURATE under this re-scoped AC.
steps.description: Structural fix half of the original slice-02. Lands the JobSubmitEvent enum WITHOUT ConvergedRunning, the WorkloadSpec discriminator wiring at the CLI submit handler, the render functions as pure-function stubs, and the anti-scenario assertion at the pure-function boundary. Closes the structural reachability of "is running with N/M replicas (took live)" for Job kind — the bug under audit cannot be emitted regardless of any runtime path because the variant the message rode on no longer exists on the JobSubmitEvent enum. Runtime end-to-end items (typed terminal, stderr-tail capture, server-side dispatch, K1 trial) are decomposed into 02-03..02-07. Depends on 01-01 (WorkloadSpec). ~1 day. **LANDED in commits f910dba + 0d5a4c2.**

steps.name: Slice 02a — TerminalCondition::Completed/Failed typed variants + match-site sweep + roundtrip proptests
steps.criteria:
AC derived from ADR-0037 Amendment 2026-05-10 (typed-terminal variants):
- crates/overdrive-core/src/transition_reason.rs: extend TerminalCondition with Completed { exit_code: i32 } and Failed { exit_code: i32 } variants. The variants derive rkyv::{Archive, Serialize, Deserialize} alongside existing variants (no breaking archive shape — new variants are additive).
- All 44 use-sites of TerminalCondition across 10 files updated for exhaustive match: every match TerminalCondition { ... } gets new arms for Completed and Failed. Sites that need to remain agnostic to exit_code use a guard or an _ catch-all only when the existing semantics genuinely treat all terminals the same.
- Roundtrip proptests: rkyv archive/deserialise roundtrip + serde JSON roundtrip for the two new variants over arbitrary i32 exit codes (proptest 1024 cases). Lives at crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/transition_reason_roundtrip.rs.
- This step is type-level only — no reconciler logic change, no observation-store schema change, no CLI render change. The runtime emission of the new variants lands in 02-04; the row-shape change lands in 02-05.

Scenarios claimed: none from distill/test-scenarios.md §2 (this is type-level plumbing). The proptest is a structural correctness check on the variant introduction itself.

Quality gates:
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core -E 'test(transition_reason_roundtrip)' GREEN
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --no-run — every use-site compiles after the exhaustive-match sweep
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace — no regressions; the 44 use-sites' existing tests still pass
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/transition_reason.rs — kill rate ≥ 80% on the new variants
steps.description: Type-level plumbing for the typed-terminal half of ADR-0037 Amendment 2026-05-10. Adds Completed { exit_code } and Failed { exit_code } variants to TerminalCondition; sweeps all 44 use-sites across 10 files for exhaustive-match correctness; proptests the rkyv + serde roundtrip. No runtime behaviour changes — every use-site remains semantically identical until 02-04 emits the new variants. Depends on 02-01 (the JobSubmitEvent enum the new TerminalCondition variants will eventually flow into). ~0.5 days.

steps.name: Slice 02b — JobLifecycle reconciler natural-exit emits typed TerminalCondition + Action variant + unit tests
steps.criteria:
AC derived from ADR-0037 Amendment 2026-05-10 (natural-exit terminal emission) + ADR-0047 §3:
- crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs (JobLifecycle::reconcile): the kind-agnostic natural-exit handler today is replaced with Job-vs-Service branching per ADR-0047 §1. On a Job-kind alloc whose terminal observation row arrives carrying exit_code, the reconciler emits a typed TerminalCondition::Completed { exit_code } when exit_code == 0, otherwise TerminalCondition::Failed { exit_code }. Service-kind preserves its existing ConvergedFailed semantics unchanged.
- New Action variant or reuse of FinalizeFailed carrying the typed TerminalCondition (not a stringified reason). The action-shim consumer in crates/overdrive-control-plane/ is updated to pass the typed terminal through to the streaming subscriber.
- The reconciler reads exit_code from the terminal observation row's existing exit_code field (the field that 02-05 ensures is populated; until 02-05 lands, ExitObserver continues writing the existing shape and the new variants flow with whatever exit_code is currently captured, which is sufficient for unit-level reconciler tests).
- Unit tests in crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs (or sibling) drive the JobLifecycle through (Pending → Running → Terminal exit 0) and (Pending → Running → Terminal exit 1) sequences; assert the emitted Action carries TerminalCondition::Completed and TerminalCondition::Failed respectively.

Scenarios claimed: none from distill/test-scenarios.md §2 directly (this is a reconciler-level behavioural change visible only at unit boundary). The user-visible outcome at S-02-01..S-02-04 lands when 02-06 wires the streaming side.

Quality gates:
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core -E 'test(job_lifecycle)' GREEN with new unit tests
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace — no regressions in existing reconciler tests
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs — kill rate ≥ 80% on the new Job-vs-Service branching
steps.description: Reconciler-level half of ADR-0037 Amendment 2026-05-10. JobLifecycle::reconcile branches on workload kind for natural-exit terminals: Job kind emits typed TerminalCondition::Completed/Failed carrying exit_code; Service kind preserves existing semantics. The action-shim path is updated to pass the typed terminal through to streaming subscribers (consumed in 02-06). Unit-level test coverage; user-visible CLI outcome lands when 02-06 wires the streaming side. Depends on 02-03 (typed variants must exist). ~0.5 days.

steps.name: Slice 02c — ExitObserver stderr-tail capture + observation-row schema field + worker test
steps.criteria:
AC derived from ADR-0033 Amendment 2026-05-10 (stderr-tail capture) + slice-02 in-AC item 3:
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/worker/exit_observer.rs (616 LoC): the worker spawns workload subprocesses via tokio::process::Command with .stderr(Stdio::piped()) and consumes stderr through a ring buffer of the last N lines (N = 5 by default, sourced from a single ExitObserver constant STDERR_TAIL_LINES). On terminal exit, the ring contents are written to the terminal observation row.
- crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs: AllocStatusRow gains a stderr_tail: Option<String> field on the terminal-row variant (or equivalent place per the existing observation-row shape). Phase-1 greenfield — no backfill code path. The field is populated by ExitObserver on Job-kind terminals; Service-kind terminals leave it None.
- format_job_failed_summary (introduced as a stub in 02-01) is updated to render stderr_tail when present — lines indented under "stderr (last N lines):". When None, render nothing.
- Worker unit/integration test exercising the stderr-pipe + ring-buffer + observation-row write path, gated behind features = ["integration-tests"] per .claude/rules/testing.md (real subprocess spawn = real-infra). Lima-routed.

Scenarios claimed: none directly from distill/test-scenarios.md §2 (the user-visible end-to-end stderr render shows up in S-02-02 once 02-06 lands). This step delivers the observation surface the render layer depends on.

Quality gates:
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'test(exit_observer_stderr_tail)' GREEN
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace — no regressions
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/worker/exit_observer.rs — kill rate ≥ 80% on the stderr-capture path
steps.description: ExitObserver stderr-tail capture (ADR-0033 Amendment 2026-05-10). Adds stderr=Stdio::piped + ring-buffer of last N lines on workload subprocesses; surfaces the tail through a new AllocStatusRow.stderr_tail field; render fn format_job_failed_summary picks it up. Phase-1 greenfield — no backfill. Integration-tests-gated worker test routed through Lima per .claude/rules/testing.md. Depends on 02-04 (the typed Failed terminal that triggers the rendering site). ~0.75 days.

steps.name: Slice 02d — Server-side build_job_stream + endpoint kind-dispatch + CLI consume_stream_job migrated to JobSubmitEvent decode
steps.criteria:
AC derived from ADR-0047 §3 / [D7] (per-kind streaming envelopes) + slice-02 in-AC item 4:
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/streaming.rs: today, build_stream emits the legacy flat SubmitEvent at line 155. Introduce build_job_stream that emits JobSubmitEvent variants (Accepted, Pending, Running, AttemptFailed, Succeeded, Failed) by reading the JobLifecycle reconciler's typed-terminal output from 02-04 + the stderr_tail field from 02-05. Service code path keeps build_stream emitting ServiceSubmitEvent unchanged.
- The streaming endpoint (HTTP submit handler) dispatches on workload kind: Service → build_stream (Service path); Job → build_job_stream; Schedule → the ScheduleSubmitEvent path from 01-02. The kind is read from the persisted WorkloadSpec discriminator at submit-stream-open time.
- crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/job.rs: consume_stream_job (line 714 today, decoding flat SubmitEvent at line 740) is rewritten to decode JobSubmitEvent. Each variant routes to its render fn from 02-01: Accepted/Pending/Running → (no per-variant render today, log line only); AttemptFailed → format_job_attempt_failed (intermediate, stream stays open); Succeeded → format_job_succeeded_summary (terminal); Failed → format_job_failed_summary including stderr_tail (terminal). The CLI process exits with the workload's exit_code on terminal: 0 on Succeeded, exit_code on Failed.
- AttemptFailed wire emission: the reconciler's per-attempt failure observation row produces an AttemptFailed event on the wire BEFORE the terminal Succeeded/Failed; consume_stream_job decodes it and prints the intermediate "attempt N failed... Retrying" line without closing the stream.
- Un-ignore the previously-#[ignore]-d streaming integration tests landed alongside the structural fix: crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_kind_streaming.rs — specifically S-02-05 (anti-scenario at the streaming integration boundary, complementing the unit-level coverage from 02-01) and S-02-06 (kind named upfront at the streaming integration boundary). If S-02-01, S-02-02, S-02-03, S-02-04, S-02-07, S-02-08 are also #[ignore]-d in this file or sibling job_submit.rs, un-ignore them in this step too.

Scenarios claimed:
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit.rs (or job_kind_streaming.rs)::S_02_01_job_exits_zero_reports_succeeded
- ::S_02_02_job_exits_nonzero_reports_failed_with_attempts (stderr-tail rendered surface)
- ::S_02_03_intermediate_attempt_failure_does_not_close_stream
- ::S_02_04_third_attempt_zero_reports_succeeded
- ::S_02_05_anti_scenario_no_is_running_with (integration-boundary manifestation)
- ::S_02_06_submit_echo_names_kind_upfront (integration-boundary manifestation)
- ::S_02_07_server_validation_failure_structured_error
- ::S_02_08_streaming_transport_interruption_surfaces_honestly

Quality gates:
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --features integration-tests -E 'binary(job_submit) | binary(job_kind_streaming)' GREEN — every un-ignored scenario passes
- cargo run -p xtask -- dst-lint exits 0 ("live" literal grep gate stays green)
- Anti-scenario assertion at the streaming integration boundary: no line of any Job-kind output contains "is running with" or "(took live)"
steps.description: Server-side per-kind streaming envelope + CLI migration to decode JobSubmitEvent. Closes the wire-format change at the build_job_stream / endpoint dispatch / consume_stream_job triple; un-ignores the streaming integration scenarios that the structural fix landed as #[ignore]-d scaffolds. CLI process exit code = workload exit code on terminal events; intermediate AttemptFailed events render without closing the stream. Depends on 02-04 (typed terminals) AND 02-05 (stderr_tail field on the row). ~1 day.

steps.name: Slice 02e — K1 100-trial Lima/ExecDriver honesty test un-ignored + GREEN
steps.criteria:
AC derived from outcome-kpis.md K1 + ADR-0047 [D2] structural mandate + slice-02 in-AC item 5:
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/coinflip_honesty_100_trials.rs: un-ignore the 100-trial Tier-3 test landed alongside the structural fix. The test runs `overdrive job submit examples/coinflip.toml` 100 times against the real ExecDriver inside Lima; for each trial it captures (cli_exit_code, kernel_observed_exit_code, terminal_verdict_line) and asserts:
  * For at least 99 of the 100 trials, cli_exit_code == kernel_observed_exit_code (KPI K1 ≥ 99% honesty).
  * For every trial, the terminal verdict line names the same exit code as the kernel observed.
  * For every trial, no line of CLI output contains "is running with" or "(took live)" (anti-scenario at the real-ExecDriver boundary).
- The test is gated behind features = ["integration-tests"] and routed exclusively through `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run` per .claude/rules/testing.md.
- Any remaining #[ignore]-d S-02-NN scenarios in job_submit.rs / job_kind_streaming.rs that 02-06 did not un-ignore (because they specifically required the real ExecDriver rather than SimDriver) are un-ignored here.

Scenarios claimed:
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/coinflip_honesty_100_trials.rs::S_02_09_K1_honesty_100_trials — Tier 3, integration-tests feature, Lima-gated, real ExecDriver
- Any S-02-NN scenarios specifically requiring real-ExecDriver execution that were not un-ignored in 02-06

Quality gates:
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --features integration-tests -E 'test(coinflip_honesty)' GREEN — ≥ 99/100 trials honest
- KPI K1 telemetry: the test records the per-trial (cli_exit, kernel_exit) tuple to a deterministic artifact path so the DELIVER reviewer can audit the 100-trial distribution
- Anti-scenario at the real-ExecDriver boundary: dst-lint stays green AND no Job-kind output line contains the forbidden substrings
steps.description: Tier-3 honesty gate. Un-ignores the K1 100-trial test against the real ExecDriver inside Lima; asserts ≥99/100 trials where CLI exit code == workload kernel exit code; reaffirms the anti-scenario at the real-ExecDriver boundary. This is the load-bearing observability KPI for the bug under audit — every prior step's structural correctness is meaningless if K1 does not pass at this scale. Depends on 02-06 (the streaming side that produces the operator-visible exit code must already be wired). ~0.5 days (mostly run time).

steps.name: Slice 03 — alloc status kind-aware Job render: Verdict, attempts table, exit codes
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from slice-03-alloc-status-job-render.md + ADR-0047 §4 / [D4] + ADR-0033 Amendment 2026-05-10; scenarios from distill/test-scenarios.md §3:
- crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs: AllocStatusRow gains kind: WorkloadKind denormalised at write time per [D4]. Phase 1 greenfield — NO backfill code path. The render layer branches once on kind without re-fetching intent.
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler.rs (or worker exit_observer write path): observation-row writer copies kind from the originally-submitted WorkloadSpec onto every AllocStatusRow it emits.
- crates/overdrive-cli/src/commands/alloc_status.rs: handler reads AllocStatusRow.kind and dispatches to the right render branch.
- crates/overdrive-cli/src/render.rs: kind-aware render branches per design [D4]. Service: header (kind: Service), Replicas (desired/running): N/M, per-alloc table columns Alloc / State / Restarts / Since, NO Exit column. Job: header (kind: Job), Verdict: { Succeeded, Failed (backoff exhausted), In progress (no terminal yet) }, per-attempt table columns Attempt / State / Exit / Started / Duration; stderr tail (last 5 lines) on Failed; Exit em-dash on Running (no terminal yet). Schedule branch already exists from 01-02 — extended to use the kind dispatcher.
- New render fns: format_job_alloc_status_header(name, kind, spec_digest, verdict), format_job_alloc_status_attempts_table(rows). The match on kind is exhaustive per ADR-0047 §1; adding a future kind means adding one match arm.

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md §3):
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/alloc_status.rs::S_03_01_service_alloc_status_replicas_no_exit_column
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/alloc_status.rs::S_03_02_job_alloc_status_failed_verdict_attempts_exit_codes_stderr — KPI K3 (the user's framing journey)
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/alloc_status.rs::S_03_03_job_alloc_status_succeeded_verdict_exit_zero
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/alloc_status.rs::S_03_04_job_alloc_status_in_progress_em_dash
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/alloc_status.rs::S_03_05_anti_scenario_job_never_renders_service_phrasing
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/alloc_status.rs::S_03_06_alloc_status_unknown_job_typed_error
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/alloc_status.rs::S_03_07_alloc_status_corrupt_observation_row_honest_error
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/alloc_status.rs::S_03_08_K3_property_rendered_exit_matches_persisted — proptest 1024 cases over exit codes {0, 1, 2, 127, 137, 255}

Quality gates:
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --features integration-tests -E 'binary(alloc_status)'
- KPI K3 automated regression: rendered Exit column byte-equals persisted exit_code for every fixture (S-03-08)
- KPI K3 manual usability gate (pre-release one-shot, 5-10 operators) per design [D9] — not gated in CI; recorded in DELIVER review
steps.description: Slice 03 of the feature — implements the user's explicit framing journey: "when we check the status using overdrive alloc status --job <id> then it should show that it failed during execution." Lands AllocStatusRow.kind denormalisation per [D4] (greenfield, no backfill); kind-aware render branches with exhaustive match; Service shows replicas (no Exit column); Job shows Verdict + per-attempt Exit codes + stderr tail; Schedule already covered by 01-02. KPI K3 automated regression test asserts byte-equality between rendered Exit column and persisted exit_code (continuous); manual usability gate is pre-release one-shot per [D9]. **Amended deps 2026-05-10**: depends on 02-03 (typed TerminalCondition variants the render Verdict reads), 02-04 (reconciler must emit the typed terminals so the AllocStatusRow shape carries the right exit_code), 02-05 (stderr_tail field on AllocStatusRow that the Job branch renders). The kind denormalisation is independent and lands in this step. ~1 day.

phases.name: Regression preservation: Service vocabulary + format_running_summary cleanup
phases.description: Mechanical regression-guard slice: renames "Job" → "Service" in format_running_summary and format_stopped_summary, removes the literal "live" duration sentinel (RCA root cause D), and migrates existing long-running test fixtures to [service] shape. Phase 03 is logically the regression check, but step-level deps put 03-01 EARLY in execution order: it is a hard predecessor of 01-03 (slice-06) because slice-06's listener-render extension extends the Service render branch this step renames. The DELIVER runner schedules on step deps, not phase-id ordering.

steps.name: Slice 04 — format_running_summary renamed to Service vocab + live literal removed + fixture sweep
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from slice-04-service-preservation.md + design Reuse Analysis (format_running_summary, format_stopped_summary) + RCA root cause D; scenarios from distill/test-scenarios.md §4:
- crates/overdrive-cli/src/render.rs: format_running_summary's only caller is the Service code path (post-WorkloadSpec discriminator). Rendered string changes from "Job '{name}' is running with N/M replicas (took {duration})" to "Service '{name}' is running with N/M replicas (took {duration})". The literal "live" is REMOVED — duration is computed from the injected Clock per the RCA Solution D fix.
- format_stopped_summary becomes kind-aware (Service / Job branches). Service: "Service '{name}' was stopped by ...". Job: "Job '{name}' was stopped by ...". Schedule: "Schedule '{name}' was deregistered by ..." (consistent with the kind dispatcher).
- Existing integration test fixtures that submit Service-shaped specs (long-running /bin/sleep 3600 etc.) migrate from the legacy flat shape to [service] + [[listener]]: crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/streaming_submit_happy_path.rs and any other test under crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/ that submits a Service-shaped spec. Single-cut migration per feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md.
- The CLI verb `overdrive job submit` is RETAINED in this feature (renaming to a kind-agnostic verb is a follow-up, NOT in scope here per design Constraints).
- dst-lint "live"-literal grep gate from 01-01 must exit 0 on the migrated codebase (S-06-03 KPI).

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md §4):
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/streaming_submit_happy_path.rs::S_04_01_service_happy_path_with_renamed_vocabulary — migrated to [service] shape
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/streaming_submit_happy_path.rs::S_04_02_service_exit_during_stability_window_emits_failed_to_stabilise
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/streaming_submit_happy_path.rs::S_04_03_anti_scenario_live_never_appears
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/streaming_submit_happy_path.rs::S_04_04_format_stopped_summary_kind_aware

Quality gates:
- cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --features integration-tests -E 'binary(streaming_submit_happy_path)'
- cargo run -p xtask -- dst-lint exits 0 (S-06-03 — "live" literal absent across the migrated codebase)
- KPI K4: existing Service happy-path tests pass with renamed vocabulary; honesty stays at 100% (Service was already correct)
steps.description: Slice 04 of the feature — Service preservation regression guard. Mechanical rename + literal removal: format_running_summary says "Service" not "Job"; the "live" duration sentinel (RCA root cause D) is replaced with a real Clock-derived value; format_stopped_summary becomes kind-aware. Existing long-running fixtures migrate to [service] shape — single-cut per feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md. Depends on 01-01 (WorkloadSpec / Service variant must exist). Hard predecessor of 01-03 (slice-06 extends this Service render branch). ~0.5 days.

validation.amendment_note: Step 02-01 split into structural-fix step (02-01, landed in commits f910dba + 0d5a4c2) plus five runtime sub-steps (02-03..02-07). Step 02-02 (slice-03) deps updated to depend on 02-03/02-04/02-05 instead of the original monolithic 02-01. Phase 02 description updated; total_steps 6 → 11. Re-review post-amendment APPROVED WITH NOTES (2026-05-10): DAG acyclic, scenario coverage preserved, sub-step ACs focused, 02-02 deps minimally sufficient. Phase-01 description prose corrected for execution-sequence ordering.

reviews.review_scope: ORIGINAL 6-step roadmap (pre-amendment). Re-review pending for the amended 11-step decomposition.

The amended 11-step roadmap's execution order is 01-01 → 03-01 → 01-02 → 02-01 → 02-03 → 02-04 → 02-05 → 02-06 → 02-07 → 02-02 → 01-03.

strengths: Slice-to-step fidelity is 100%: every slice outcome maps to roadmap acceptance criteria
Dependency graph is acyclic and topologically valid; cross-phase deps correctly declared at step level
All 6 steps declare explicit, measurable quality gates (KPI assertions, acceptance test invocations, feature gates)
AC is strictly behavioral, never implementation-coupled; no underscore-prefixed identifiers or method signatures

Walking-skeleton coverage present (WS-01..WS-04); driving-port invocation explicit (parser, CLI handlers)

findings.label: informational
findings.concern: Criteria word count (~8.3x threshold) exceeds numeric guidelines
findings.resolution: Project policy in CLAUDE.md explicitly overrides word-count warnings for scenario-specific AC. No action needed.

reviews.verdict_summary: APPROVED - roadmap is ready for DELIVER wave execution. No blocking issues. Scenario traceability enables Outside-In TDD.
